‚Äã 

‚ÄãStandard Operation Procedure 

‚ÄãFor Emergency Safety Eyewash and Shower Stations 

‚ÄãBradley,  Eyewash Serial Number Z358-1-209 and Bradley, Shower , Serial Number 114-050 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãIn the University of Maryland Greenebaum Comprehensive Cancer Center Flow Cytometry Shared Services, UMGCCC FCSS 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãBressler Research Building, Rooms BRB 7-010 and BRB 7-022 

‚Äã‚ÄãPrepared by:  Underwood, Karen‚Äã 

‚Äã‚Äã1-29-2020‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã
 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãTable of Contents 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãSection										Page Number	 

‚Äã1.0	Object 										2 

‚Äã2.0	Scope											2 

‚Äã3.0	Responsibilities									2 

‚Äã4.0	Definitions										3 

‚Äã5.0	Purpose										3 

‚Äã6.0	Procedure										3 

‚Äã7.0	Testing/ Maintenance								4  

‚Äã8.0	References										5 

‚Äã 

‚Äã
 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãStandard Operation Procedure for Emergency Eye Wash and Shower Equipment 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãObject: 

‚ÄãThe object of this SOP is to establish the minimum performance requirements for the eye wash and shower equipment, for emergency treatment of the eyes or body of an employee, student, and/or volunteer who has been exposed to dangerous material.  The shower is in Bressler Research Building, (BRB), 7-010 and the Eye Wash is in BRB 7-022.  Both areas are in University of Maryland Greenebaum Comprehensive Cancer Center Flow Cytometry Shared Services, (UMGCCC FCSS).  Dr. Xiaoxuan Fan is the PI and Karen F. Underwood Lab Manager/Research Lead Specialist of the facility. 

‚ÄãScope: 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãThis standard establishes the minimum performance requirements for eye wash and shower equipment for the emergency treatment of the eyes or body of an employee, student, and volunteer who has been exposed to injurious materials. It covers the following types of equipment: emergency showers, and eyewash station. 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãPlumbed eyewash unit is provided for emergencies, the eyes of an employee, student or visitor may encounter any substance which can cause corrosion, severe irritation, or permanent tissue damage or which is toxic by absorption.  If there is any possibility that an individual‚Äôs eyes may be splashed with cryogenic materials the lab has to provide acceptable eyewash facilities within the immediate work area for emergency use.  The eyewash station in BRB 7-022 is available during working hours, and there is another station available in BRB 7-010 24 hours. 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãEmergency shower shall be provided for all work areas in the case of emergencies.  Provided areas of the body may encounter any substance which can cause corrosion, severe irritation, or permanent tissue damage or which is toxic by absorption.  The shower is available 24 hours in BRB 7-010. 

‚Äã 

‚Äã3.0  Responsibilities: 

‚Äã3.1  	Principal Investigator:  The Principal Investigator, PI, is responsible for ensuring  

‚Äãpersonnel under their supervision are trained on the safe and proper use of the BSC and that correct procedures are followed.  

‚Äã 

‚Äã3.2  	Designated person: The PI may designate a member of the lab to oversee the correct operation and maintenance of the safety eyewash and showers. This person shall:  

‚Äã 

‚Äã 	 

‚ÄãOversee the weekly testing and documentation of plumbed eyewash is up to date. 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãEnsure the eyewash and shower stations and area around the equipment stations are kept clear. 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãOversee that University of Maryland Baltimore Facilities comes out for the quarterly testing and documentation of the safety showers are kept up to date. 

‚Äã 

‚Äã4.0	Definitions: 

‚Äã4.1	Emergency Shower: A device which delivers flushing fluid that utilizes a valve which remains open during use to enable the user to have water cascading over the entire body while the hands are free. 

‚Äã4.2	Eyewash: a unit that flushes water specifically to the eyes.  It is designed to provide water to a person that has splashed corrosive or toxic material in their eye(s).   

‚Äã4. 3	Hazardous/Injurious Material: any substance that could cause corrosion, severe irritation, or permanent tissue damage or is toxic by absorption. 

‚Äã 

‚Äã5.0	Purpose: 

‚Äã5.1	An eyewash station is a piece of equipment stationed within a reasonable distance of the laboratory and is not blocked (ensure easy access).  This is used if there is a splash of corrosive or toxic material in a person‚Äôs eyes.  The eyewash is from Bradley, serial number Z358-1-209 and the location is in BRB 7-022.   

‚Äã 

‚Äã5.2	A safety shower is a piece of equipment that is designed to provide over 30 gallons of water per minute to a person that has spilled corrosive or toxic material on their body.  The university requires that a safety shower is within a reasonable distance of the laboratory and that there is access to it (it is not blocked.)  ‚ÄúEnvironmental Health and Safety University of Maryland Baltimore‚Äù  The emergency shower is Bradley, serial number 114-050 and is in BRB 7-010.   

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã6.0	Procedure: 

‚Äã 

‚Äã6.1	Usage 

‚Äãa.	Begin flushing as quickly as possible after the eye encounters a harmful substance as the first 10 seconds are critical. Toxic substances, when meeting the eye, immediately begin to damage sensitive eye tissues. The longer they remain in contact, the greater the damage to the eye. Besides tissue damage, acids and alkali can change the pH in the eye itself. When the pH of the eye begins to get out of the narrow tolerable range, severe eye damage, including blindness, may result. 

‚Äã 

‚Äãb.	In the event that the body encounters corrosive and/or dangerous material an emergency shower is provided.   

‚Äã 

‚Äã6.2	General Check List for Eye Wash 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãIf chemical and/or dangerous material gets in the eyes notify flow core staff immediately and go to eyewash station. 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãHold your eyes open with your fingers. 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãBegin flushing by pressing the lever on the back of the unit. 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãRoll your eyes and tilt your head so water is darning away from your eyes. 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãFlush for a full 15 to 20 minutes. 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãIf you have contacts remove them ASAP 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãSeek medical attention at University Maryland Medical Center 

‚Äã 

‚Äã6.3	General Check List for Emergency Shower 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãImmediately notify flow core staff, if during business hours, and flush the affected area with copious amounts of water for at least 15 to 20 minutes. 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãRemove contaminated clothing, shoes, jewelry, etc. 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãCall Environmental Health and Safety at 410-706-7055, if after hours call campus police 911. 

‚Äã 

‚Äã7.0	Testing/Maintenance  

‚Äã 

‚Äã7.1		Eyewash stations are tested every week, by running the station for 15 minutes  

‚Äãonce a week.  This is done by Flow Core Staff for BRB 7-022 Eyewash Station, and by Center of Innovative Biomedical Resources Staff for eyewash in BRB 7-010.   

‚Äã7.2           	Eyewash inspection logs are maintained at or near the eyewash station(s) by the appropriate staff mentioned above. 

‚Äã 

‚Äã7.3	Shower is tested by Facilities Management quarterly.  Following their protocol for inspection. 

‚Äã 

‚Äã7.4	Safety shower inspection logs are maintained at or near the shower site by Facilities Management. 

‚Äã 

‚Äã8.0	References 

‚Äã 

‚Äã8.1	Environmental Health and Safety at University of Maryland Baltimore: 

‚Äãhttps://www.umaryland.edu/ehs/programs/laboratory-audit-program/eye-wash-stations/ 

‚Äã 

‚Äã8.2	Bradley, A Guide to the ANSI/ISEA Z358.1-2014 Standard for Emergency Eyewashes and Shower Equipment:  https://www.bradleycorp.com/mediamanager/view/20293/Bradley_Safety_ANSIGuide.pdf 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãWritten by:		Karen F. Underwood, MS, Research Lead Specialist 

‚ÄãAdapted by:		 

‚ÄãEffective date:	 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãApproved by:	______________________________		___________________ 

‚ÄãXiaxouan Fan, Ph.D., Director			Date 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãReviewed by:		_______________________________		____________________ 

‚ÄãDate 

‚Äã 

‚Äã_______________________________		____________________ 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãDate 

‚Äã_______________________________		____________________ 

‚ÄãDate 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãMODIFICATIONS AFTER ORIGINAL IMPLEMENTATION 

‚Äã 

‚ÄãDate: 

‚ÄãDescription of Modification: 

‚ÄãApproved by: 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã 

‚Äã
‚Äã
